{"id":277,"date":"2026-06-26T04:12:27","date_gmt":"2026-06-26T04:12:27","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/beesayatv.test\/?post_type=trail&p=277"},"modified":"2026-07-14T10:01:03","modified_gmt":"2026-07-14T10:01:03","slug":"camp-7-lubas-farm-trails","status":"publish","type":"trail","link":"https:\/\/beesayatv.test\/trails\/camp-7-lubas-farm-trails\/","title":{"rendered":"Camp 7 Farmland Trails | Minglanilla Part 1| 2026 GPX"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

Camp 7 Farm Trails is a point-to-point hike through the farmland of Camp 7, Lubas. The route follows rolling countryside, gradual elevation changes, and two small river crossings before finishing at the endpoint. The terrain is generally gentle and non-technical, making it suitable for beginners, although much of the hike is exposed to the sun.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
EASY<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>May 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>6.07 km (Point-to-Point)<\/strong><\/div>

\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>1.5 - 2 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 2 liters<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>100% trail<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Supporter Access<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began from a roadside location identified in the GPX track. Motorcycle parking was available along the road, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. The jump-off was reached via a concrete road. Private vehicles could access the area, while public transportation was limited. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Easy<\/h3>
\u2713 Easy-to-follow trail<\/div>
\u2713 No technical climbing required<\/div>
\u2713 Short hiking distance<\/div>
\u2713 Mostly downhill route<\/div>
\u2713 River crossings are simple<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> farmland<\/a> \u2022<\/span> forest<\/a> \u2022<\/span> river crossing<\/a> \u2022<\/span> viewpoint<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The route passes through elevated farmland with mostly gentle to moderate uphill sections before transitioning into a longer downhill stretch toward the end of the hike. The terrain rolls gradually through open countryside and includes two small river crossings along the way.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Although the elevation changes are moderate, the extended downhill section may feel tiring near the end of the route. Most of the trail is exposed, with very little shade available.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The trail was dry at the time but can become muddy during rainy weather. Shade coverage was very limited, and no stores were encountered along the route.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was strong near the start before gradually weakening farther along the trail. At least 2 liters of water was recommended. Very few people and very few insects were encountered during the hike.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The hike begins on a road that was under construction during the documented hike. This section can become very muddy after rain.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

The route crosses two small rivers. One crossing may become significantly deeper after heavy rainfall. At the time of recording (May 2026), a cement bridge was under construction at this location.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
